demographics.living_situation: newborn living at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — my baby is only 6 days old. He throws up every single time he nurses, his skin turned yellow overnight, and now I can barely wake him up. He's gone all floppy.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been getting worse fast over the last day — he won't feed, he's really sleepy and limp, and he feels cold. We don't know what's wrong.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about 24 to 36 hours ago and has got much worse in the last few hours
symptom_narrative.duration: worsening over a day; he became very hard to wake about 4 hours ago
symptom_narrative.character: he vomits within a minute or two of every breastfeed, his skin has gone yellow and the yellow is spreading down his body, and he has become very sleepy, floppy and weak
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst he has been — I can hardly wake him and his cry is weak
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the yellow colour is on his face and eyes and now his tummy; the sleepiness and floppiness is his whole body
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: vomiting comes with every feed; the sleepiness has been constant and getting deeper
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: he brings up milk every time we feed him and seems to get more sleepy afterwards
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he went jittery and shaky for a moment after his last feed, he feels cold to touch, and some tiny red-purple spots have come up on his chest
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: none — he seemed well when he came home from hospital after birth
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is barely feeding and is very hard to rouse

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: born at term after a normal pregnancy and delivery; no problems until this

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 6-day-old newborn at home; both parents present; mother is breastfeeding and was trying to feed him when she called

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: newborn; parents report he fed and behaved normally for the first few days, then rapidly deteriorated

objective_data_offered.value: his skin and the whites of his eyes turned yellow overnight and the yellow has spread down to his tummy
objective_data_offered.timing: started overnight, spreading over the last day

objective_data_offered.value: I can barely wake him, he's gone really floppy and limp and his cry is weak
objective_data_offered.timing: much worse over the last 4 hours

objective_data_offered.value: he vomits within a minute or two every time he breastfeeds and now won't feed properly at all

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parental touch impression, not a thermometer reading

objective_data_offered.value: tiny red-purple spots have appeared on his chest
he went jittery and shaky for a short time right after his last feed
